# CloudFront Distribution Configuration - Complete Property Documentation

## Overview
CloudFormation template will create **2 CloudFront distributions** (one per region) with identical configuration serving all 6 custom domains through a single global CDN per region.

---

## Distribution Names & IDs

### Primary Region (us-east-1)
**Comment:** `Multi-Service Distribution - prod - us-east-1`
**Distribution ID:** Generated by CloudFormation (e.g., E1A2B3C4D5E6F7)
**Domain Name:** Generated by CloudFormation (e.g., d123abc456def.cloudfront.net)

### DR Region (us-west-2)
**Comment:** `Multi-Service Distribution - prod - us-west-2`
**Distribution ID:** Generated by CloudFormation (e.g., E7F6E5D4C3B2A1)
**Domain Name:** Generated by CloudFormation (e.g., d789ghi012jkl.cloudfront.net)

**Note:** CloudFront is a global service, but we create separate distributions per region because each points to regional S3 origins. Route 53 DNS failover switches between distributions.

---

## Core Configuration Properties

### 1. Enabled
```yaml
Enabled: true
```
- **Status:** Active and serving traffic immediately after deployment
- **Effect:** Distribution starts accepting requests as soon as deployed
- **Deployment Time:** 15-20 minutes for initial propagation to edge locations

### 2. HttpVersion
```yaml
HttpVersion: http2
```
- **Protocol:** HTTP/2 enabled
- **Benefits:**
  - Multiplexing (multiple requests over single connection)
  - Header compression
  - Server push capability (not used currently)
  - Faster page loads
- **Fallback:** Automatically downgrades to HTTP/1.1 for older clients

### 3. IPV6Enabled
```yaml
IPV6Enabled: true
```
- **IPv6 Support:** Enabled
- **Effect:** CloudFront responds to both IPv4 and IPv6 requests
- **DNS:** Both A (IPv4) and AAAA (IPv6) records available
- **Coverage:** Supports modern networks and mobile carriers

### 4. Comment
```yaml
Comment: !Sub 'Multi-Service Distribution - ${Environment} - ${Region}'
```
- **us-east-1:** "Multi-Service Distribution - prod - us-east-1"
- **us-west-2:** "Multi-Service Distribution - prod - us-west-2"
- **Purpose:** Human-readable description for AWS Console identification

---

## Custom Domain Names (Aliases)

### 6 Custom Domains Configured

```yaml
Aliases:
  - admin.prod.gsa.dos.macp.cloud
  - agent.prod.gsa.dos.macp.cloud
  - health.prod.gsa.dos.macp.cloud
  - chat.prod.gsa.dos.macp.cloud
  - config.prod.gsa.dos.macp.cloud
  - chat-api.prod.gsa.dos.macp.cloud
```

**Domain Purposes:**
- **admin.*** → Admin portal redirect page
- **agent.*** → CCP/Agent workspace application
- **health.*** → Health check endpoint (future API Gateway)
- **chat.*** → Chat widget static assets
- **config.*** → Configuration API (future API Gateway)
- **chat-api.*** → Chat backend API (future API Gateway)

**Requirements for Custom Domains:**
- ACM certificate in us-east-1 covering all 6 domains
- DNS CNAME or Alias records pointing to CloudFront domain
- Certificate must be validated before CloudFront deployment

---

## Origins Configuration

### Current: 3 S3 Origins (Static Content)

#### Origin 1: AdminS3Origin
```yaml
Id: AdminS3Origin
DomainName: macp-prod-us-east-1-cloudfront-content.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com
OriginPath: /admin
OriginAccessControlId: [OAC-ID]
S3OriginConfig: {}
ConnectionAttempts: 3
ConnectionTimeout: 10
```
- **Purpose:** Serves admin portal redirect page
- **Path Prefix:** `/admin` - CloudFront prepends to all requests
- **Example:** Request to `admin.prod.../index.html` → fetches `/admin/index.html` from S3
- **Retry:** 3 attempts on connection failure
- **Timeout:** 10 seconds per connection attempt

#### Origin 2: AgentS3Origin
```yaml
Id: AgentS3Origin
DomainName: macp-prod-us-east-1-cloudfront-content.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com
OriginPath: /agent
OriginAccessControlId: [OAC-ID]
S3OriginConfig: {}
ConnectionAttempts: 3
ConnectionTimeout: 10
```
- **Purpose:** Serves CCP/Agent workspace application
- **Path Prefix:** `/agent`
- **Status:** Ready for content deployment

#### Origin 3: ChatS3Origin
```yaml
Id: ChatS3Origin
DomainName: macp-prod-us-east-1-cloudfront-content.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com
OriginPath: /chat
OriginAccessControlId: [OAC-ID]
S3OriginConfig: {}
ConnectionAttempts: 3
ConnectionTimeout: 10
```
- **Purpose:** Serves chat widget static assets
- **Path Prefix:** `/chat`
- **Status:** Ready for content deployment

### Future: 3 API Gateway Origins (APIs)

**Placeholder for future deployment:**
```yaml
# - Id: ChatAPIOrigin
#   DomainName: [api-gateway-id].execute-api.us-east-1.amazonaws.com
#   CustomOriginConfig: {...}
#   
# - Id: ConfigAPIOrigin
#   DomainName: [api-gateway-id].execute-api.us-east-1.amazonaws.com
#   CustomOriginConfig: {...}
#   
# - Id: HealthAPIOrigin
#   DomainName: [api-gateway-id].execute-api.us-east-1.amazonaws.com
#   CustomOriginConfig: {...}
```

**To be added when:**
- Lambda functions deployed
- API Gateway created
- Endpoints tested and validated

---

## Origin Access Control (OAC)

### Configuration
```yaml
OriginAccessControlConfig:
  Name: OAC-prod-us-east-1-multiservice
  OriginAccessControlOriginType: s3
  SigningBehavior: always
  SigningProtocol: sigv4
```

**Properties:**
- **Type:** S3 (for S3 bucket origins)
- **Signing:** SigV4 - AWS signature version 4
- **Behavior:** Always sign requests
- **Security:** Only CloudFront can access S3 bucket (enforced by bucket policy)

**OAC vs OAI (Origin Access Identity):**
- **OAC:** Modern, recommended approach (what we're using)
- **OAI:** Legacy approach (deprecated)
- **Benefits of OAC:**
  - Supports all S3 buckets (including SSE-KMS encryption)
  - Better security with SigV4 signing
  - Works with S3 Object Ownership (BucketOwnerEnforced)
  - Required for modern S3 configurations

---

## Default Cache Behavior

```yaml
DefaultCacheBehavior:
  TargetOriginId: AdminS3Origin
  ViewerProtocolPolicy: redirect-to-https
  AllowedMethods:
    - GET
    - HEAD
  CachedMethods:
    - GET
    - HEAD
  Compress: true
  CachePolicyId: 658327ea-f89d-4fab-a63d-7e88639e58f6
```

### TargetOriginId: AdminS3Origin
- **Default Origin:** Admin content
- **Fallback:** All requests not matching specific cache behaviors go here
- **Current Usage:** Serves all 6 domains (no additional cache behaviors yet)

### ViewerProtocolPolicy: redirect-to-https
- **HTTP Requests:** Automatically redirected to HTTPS (301 redirect)
- **HTTPS Requests:** Served directly
- **Effect:** Forces encryption for all traffic
- **Client Impact:** Single redirect for HTTP users

### AllowedMethods: GET, HEAD
- **GET:** Retrieve content
- **HEAD:** Retrieve headers only (no body)
- **Not Allowed:** POST, PUT, DELETE, OPTIONS, PATCH
- **Use Case:** Read-only static content distribution
- **Note:** API Gateway origins may need additional methods (to be configured later)

### CachedMethods: GET, HEAD
- **GET:** Responses cached at edge locations
- **HEAD:** Responses cached at edge locations
- **Effect:** Reduces origin load, improves latency
- **TTL:** Controlled by cache policy

### Compress: true
- **Compression:** Automatic gzip/brotli compression
- **File Types:** Text-based files (HTML, CSS, JS, JSON, XML, etc.)
- **Min Size:** Typically 1000 bytes minimum
- **Benefits:**
  - Faster downloads (smaller file sizes)
  - Reduced bandwidth costs
  - Improved page load times

### CachePolicyId: 658327ea-f89d-4fab-a63d-7e88639e58f6
- **Policy:** AWS Managed - CachingOptimized
- **TTL:** 
  - Min: 1 second
  - Default: 86400 seconds (24 hours)
  - Max: 31536000 seconds (365 days)
- **Headers:** None forwarded to origin (optimized caching)
- **Query Strings:** None forwarded (optimized caching)
- **Cookies:** None forwarded (optimized caching)
- **Compression:** Enabled (accepts gzip, brotli)

**Cache Policy Details:**
- Optimized for static content that doesn't change frequently
- Maximum cache hit ratio
- Minimal origin requests
- Suitable for HTML, CSS, JS, images

---

## Additional Cache Behaviors

```yaml
CacheBehaviors:
  Quantity: 0
```

**Current Configuration:** None

**Why No Additional Behaviors?**
- All 6 domains currently route to same origin (AdminS3Origin)
- CloudFront uses Host header to differentiate subdomains
- Default behavior handles all traffic

**Future Considerations:**
When API Gateway origins are added, may need path-based routing:
```yaml
# Example future cache behavior for APIs
- PathPattern: /api/*
  TargetOriginId: ChatAPIOrigin
  ViewerProtocolPolicy: https-only
  AllowedMethods: [GET, HEAD, OPTIONS, PUT, POST, PATCH, DELETE]
  CachePolicyId: 4135ea2d-6df8-44a3-9df3-4b5a84be39ad  # CachingDisabled
```

---

## SSL/TLS Configuration (Viewer Certificate)

```yaml
ViewerCertificate:
  AcmCertificateArn: arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:417886991978:certificate/e925eda1-90b5-4607-8ad2-c2e924a3bb71
  SslSupportMethod: sni-only
  MinimumProtocolVersion: TLSv1.3_2025
```

### ACM Certificate
- **ARN:** arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:417886991978:certificate/e925eda1-90b5-4607-8ad2-c2e924a3bb71
- **Region:** Must be in us-east-1 (CloudFront requirement)
- **Domains Covered:** *.prod.gsa.dos.macp.cloud or explicit 6 domains
- **Validation:** Must be validated before CloudFront deployment
- **Cost:** Free (AWS Certificate Manager)

### SSL Support Method: sni-only
- **SNI (Server Name Indication):** Enabled
- **Cost:** Free
- **Client Support:** All modern browsers and clients (2010+)
- **Not Supported:** Very old clients (IE on Windows XP, Android 2.x)
- **Alternative:** Dedicated IP ($600/month per distribution) - not needed

### Minimum Protocol Version: TLSv1.3_2025
- **TLS Version:** 1.3 minimum (most secure)
- **Released:** August 2018, widely adopted
- **Benefits:**
  - Faster handshakes (fewer round trips)
  - Forward secrecy
  - Removed weak cipher suites
  - Better privacy (encrypted handshake)
- **Client Support:** Modern browsers (Chrome 70+, Firefox 63+, Safari 12.1+)
- **Trade-off:** May exclude very old clients (acceptable for most use cases)

**TLS Version Comparison:**
- TLSv1.3_2025 (Most secure, our choice)
- TLSv1.2_2021
- TLSv1.2_2019
- TLSv1.2_2018
- TLSv1.1_2016 (Deprecated)
- TLSv1 (Deprecated)

---

## Geographic Restrictions

```yaml
Restrictions:
  GeoRestriction:
    RestrictionType: whitelist
    Locations:
      - US
```

### RestrictionType: whitelist
- **Access:** Only allowed countries can access
- **Blocked Countries:** All except those explicitly listed
- **Enforcement:** At CloudFront edge locations (before origin request)
- **Response:** 403 Forbidden for blocked countries

### Locations: US
- **Allowed:** United States only
- **ISO Code:** US
- **Effect:** All requests from outside US are blocked
- **Use Case:** Compliance requirement for government/federal data
- **Detection:** Based on IP geolocation (99%+ accurate)

**Alternative Options (not used):**
- `blacklist` - Block specific countries, allow all others
- `none` - No geographic restrictions

---

## Web Application Firewall (WAF)

```yaml
WebACLId: arn:aws:wafv2:us-east-1:417886991978:global/webacl/FMManagedWebACLV2-mms-waf-acl-US-Only-fortinet-1776365461408/0e665655-35a1-448e-bfaa-e1fc3a90ac70
```

### WAF Configuration
- **Service:** AWS WAF v2
- **Type:** Managed Web ACL
- **Name:** FMManagedWebACLV2-mms-waf-acl-US-Only-fortinet-1776365461408
- **Region:** Global (us-east-1 for CloudFront)
- **Management:** Firewall Manager (FMM)

**WAF Protection Includes:**
- DDoS protection
- SQL injection prevention
- Cross-site scripting (XSS) protection
- Rate limiting
- Bot detection
- Known malicious IP blocking
- Geographic enforcement (complements CloudFront geo-restriction)

**Cost:** 
- WAF charges apply (per Web ACL, per rule, per million requests)
- Managed by Firewall Manager across organization

---

## Access Logging

```yaml
Logging:
  Enabled: true
  IncludeCookies: false
  Bucket: maximus-cloudfront-logs-417886991978-us-east-1.s3.amazonaws.com
  Prefix: prod-us-east-1-admin-portal/
```

### Log Destination
- **S3 Bucket:** maximus-cloudfront-logs-417886991978-us-east-1
- **Format:** S3 bucket domain name (required format)
- **Same for both regions:** Both us-east-1 and us-west-2 distributions log to us-east-1 bucket

### Log Prefix
- **us-east-1:** `prod-us-east-1-admin-portal/`
- **us-west-2:** `prod-us-west-2-admin-portal/`
- **Separates:** Logs from different regions

### Log Format
**File Name Pattern:**
```
[prefix]/[distribution-id].YYYY-MM-DD-HH.unique-id.gz
```

**Example:**
```
prod-us-east-1-admin-portal/E1A2B3C4D5E6F7.2026-04-17-20.ab12cd34.gz
```

### Log Fields (Standard CloudFront Access Logs)
Includes ~30+ fields:
- Date and time
- Edge location (e.g., IAD89-C1)
- Bytes sent to client
- Client IP address
- HTTP method (GET, POST, etc.)
- Host (custom domain)
- URI stem (path)
- HTTP status code (200, 404, etc.)
- Referrer
- User-Agent
- Query string
- Cookie (if IncludeCookies=true)
- Edge result type (Hit, Miss, Error, etc.)
- Request ID
- TLS protocol version
- Cipher suite
- And more...

### IncludeCookies: false
- **Cookies:** Not logged
- **Privacy:** Prevents sensitive cookie data in logs
- **Use Case:** Cookies not needed for static content analysis

**Alternative:** Set to `true` if cookie analysis needed (e.g., session tracking)

---

## Price Class

```yaml
PriceClass: PriceClass_All
```

### Available Price Classes

| Class | Edge Locations | Cost | Our Choice |
|-------|---------------|------|-----------|
| PriceClass_All | All edge locations worldwide (200+) | Highest | ✅ Selected |
| PriceClass_200 | Most edge locations (excludes South America, Australia) | Medium | ❌ |
| PriceClass_100 | North America, Europe only | Lowest | ❌ |

### PriceClass_All Details
- **Coverage:** 200+ edge locations across 90+ cities in 48+ countries
- **Regions:** 
  - North America
  - Europe
  - Asia
  - South America
  - Australia
  - Middle East
  - Africa
- **Latency:** Best possible (closest edge to users)
- **Cost:** Highest per-GB data transfer (varies by region)
- **Trade-off:** Maximum performance vs. cost

**Why All?**
- Government users may be deployed globally (embassies, military bases)
- Best user experience regardless of location
- Geo-restriction still enforces US-only access, but serving from nearest edge

---

## Origin Groups (Failover)

```yaml
OriginGroups:
  Quantity: 0
```

**Current:** Not configured

**What are Origin Groups?**
- CloudFront feature for origin-level failover
- Primary origin fails (5xx errors) → automatic retry to secondary origin
- Would allow single CloudFront with multi-region S3 failover

**Why not using?**
- Using Route 53 DNS failover instead (switches between two CloudFront distributions)
- Provides more control (manual approval via health check manipulation)
- Aligns with semi-automatic DR strategy in the plan

**Future Consideration:**
Could add origin groups for automatic failover within single distribution:
```yaml
OriginGroups:
  - Id: AdminOriginGroup
    Members:
      - OriginId: AdminS3Origin-East
      - OriginId: AdminS3Origin-West
    FailoverCriteria:
      StatusCodes: [500, 502, 503, 504]
```

---

## Lambda@Edge & CloudFront Functions

### Lambda@Edge
```yaml
LambdaFunctionAssociations:
  Quantity: 0
```
**Current:** None configured

**Potential Use Cases (Future):**
- URL rewriting/redirects
- A/B testing
- Authentication headers
- Custom error pages
- Security headers injection

### CloudFront Functions
```yaml
FunctionAssociations:
  Quantity: 0
```
**Current:** None configured

**Potential Use Cases (Future):**
- Simple URL rewrites (cheaper than Lambda@Edge)
- Request header manipulation
- Cache key normalization
- Viewer request/response modification

**Difference:**
- **CloudFront Functions:** Lightweight, JavaScript, sub-millisecond, viewer request/response only
- **Lambda@Edge:** Full Lambda, Node.js/Python, origin request/response, more powerful

---

## Custom Error Responses

```yaml
CustomErrorResponses:
  Quantity: 0
```

**Current:** Not configured

**Default Behavior:**
- 4xx errors (404, 403): Passed through from origin to viewer
- 5xx errors (500, 502, 503): Passed through from origin to viewer

**Future Enhancement:**
Could add custom error pages:
```yaml
CustomErrorResponses:
  - ErrorCode: 404
    ResponsePagePath: /errors/404.html
    ResponseCode: 404
    ErrorCachingMinTTL: 300
  - ErrorCode: 403
    ResponsePagePath: /errors/403.html
    ResponseCode: 403
    ErrorCachingMinTTL: 60
```

---

## Properties NOT Configured

### ❌ DefaultRootObject
- **Not set:** Empty string
- **Effect:** No default document
- **Behavior:** Request to `https://admin.../` does NOT automatically serve `index.html`
- **Must specify:** `https://admin.../index.html` explicitly
- **Reason:** Not needed for single-page redirects; can be added later

### ❌ SmoothStreaming
- **Not enabled:** false
- **Purpose:** Microsoft Smooth Streaming media format
- **Not needed:** No video streaming use case

### ❌ Trusted Signers / Trusted Key Groups
- **Not configured**
- **Purpose:** Signed URLs/cookies for private content
- **Not needed:** All content is public (within geo-restriction)

### ❌ Field-Level Encryption
- **Not configured**
- **Purpose:** Encrypt specific fields at edge before forwarding to origin
- **Not needed:** No sensitive form data

### ❌ Real-time Logs
- **Not configured**
- **Alternative:** Using standard CloudFront access logs
- **Difference:** Real-time logs stream to Kinesis (seconds), standard logs batch to S3 (minutes/hours)

### ❌ Origin Shield
- **Not enabled**
- **Purpose:** Additional caching layer between edge and origin
- **Cost:** $0.01 per 10,000 requests
- **Not needed:** Origin (S3) can handle current load

### ❌ Continuous Deployment
- **Not configured**
- **Purpose:** Blue/green testing for distribution changes
- **Not needed:** Using separate dev/staging environments

---

## Tags

```yaml
Tags:
  - Key: Environment
    Value: prod
  - Key: Region
    Value: us-east-1  (or us-west-2)
  - Key: Purpose
    Value: Multi-Service Distribution (admin, agent, chat, APIs)
```

**Limited Tags:**
- CloudFront distributions have fewer tags than S3 buckets
- Primarily for identification and cost allocation
- Could add more organizational tags if needed

---

## Deployment Timing

### Initial Deployment
```
CloudFormation Stack Creation: ~2-3 minutes
├── S3 Bucket: 30 seconds
├── OAC: 10 seconds
├── CloudFront Distribution: 15-20 minutes ⏰
└── Stack Complete: ~20 minutes total
```

**Why So Long?**
- CloudFront must propagate configuration to 200+ edge locations worldwide
- Cannot be accelerated
- Status shows as "In Progress" during propagation

### Updates to Existing Distribution
```
Configuration Change: 5-15 minutes
```
- Depends on change type
- Most changes require edge propagation
- Some changes are faster (logging, tags)

### Disabling/Deleting
```
Disable: 15-20 minutes
Delete: 15-20 minutes (must be disabled first)
Total: ~40 minutes to fully remove
```

---

## Routing & Traffic Flow

### How Requests Are Routed

1. **User Request:** `https://admin.prod.gsa.dos.macp.cloud/index.html`

2. **DNS Resolution (Route 53):**
   - Returns CloudFront distribution domain
   - Failover logic determines primary vs DR distribution

3. **CloudFront Edge Location:**
   - User connects to nearest edge location (based on geography)
   - Geo-restriction check (US only)
   - WAF rules evaluated

4. **Cache Check:**
   - Hit: Serve from edge cache (fastest)
   - Miss: Request from origin

5. **Origin Request (if cache miss):**
   - CloudFront → S3 bucket (using OAC)
   - Request: `/admin/index.html`
   - S3 bucket policy verifies CloudFront OAC

6. **Response:**
   - S3 → CloudFront → User
   - Object cached at edge (per cache policy TTL)
   - Subsequent requests served from cache

### Multi-Domain Routing

**Same CloudFront Distribution Serves All 6 Domains:**

Request to different domains:
- `admin.prod...` → Uses Host header, serves from AdminS3Origin
- `agent.prod...` → Uses Host header, serves from AdminS3Origin (default)
- `chat.prod...` → Uses Host header, serves from AdminS3Origin (default)

**Note:** Currently all domains route to AdminS3Origin. Future cache behaviors will route different paths to different origins (API Gateway origins).

---

## Performance Characteristics

### Cache Hit Ratio
- **Target:** >85% for static content
- **Depends On:**
  - Cache policy TTL
  - Content change frequency
  - Traffic patterns
  - Query string variations

### Latency
- **Cache Hit:** 10-50ms (edge to user)
- **Cache Miss (first request):** 100-300ms (edge → origin → edge → user)
- **Subsequent Requests:** Cache hit (10-50ms)

### Throughput
- **Per Edge Location:** Scales automatically
- **Global Capacity:** Massive (AWS infrastructure)
- **No Limits:** Pay-as-you-go scaling

### Availability
- **SLA:** 99.9% availability
- **Edge Redundancy:** Multiple edge servers per location
- **Origin Failover:** Via Route 53 DNS failover to DR distribution

---

## Security Summary

| Feature | Status | Protection Against |
|---------|--------|-------------------|
| HTTPS Enforcement | ✅ Enforced | Man-in-the-middle attacks |
| TLS 1.3 | ✅ Minimum | Protocol downgrade attacks |
| Origin Access Control | ✅ Enabled | Direct S3 access bypass |
| Geo-Restriction | ✅ US Only | Unauthorized geographic access |
| WAF | ✅ Enabled | OWASP Top 10, DDoS, bots |
| ACM Certificate | ✅ Configured | Certificate management |
| SNI | ✅ Enabled | Cost-effective SSL |
| IPv6 | ✅ Enabled | Modern network support |

---

## Cost Estimate (Per Distribution)

### Monthly Cost Breakdown

**Assumptions:**
- 100GB data transfer out
- 1 million requests
- US/Canada/Europe traffic primarily

```
Data Transfer Out:
  First 10 TB: $0.085/GB × 100 GB = $8.50

HTTPS Requests:
  First 10M: $0.0100 per 10,000 × 100 = $1.00

SSL/TLS Certificates:
  ACM Certificate: Free

Origin Requests (cache misses @ 15%):
  150,000 requests × $0.0075 per 10,000 = $1.13

Invalidations:
  Assume 10 paths/month, first 1000 free = $0.00

Total per Distribution: ~$10.63/month
```

**For 2 Distributions:** ~$21/month

**Additional Costs:**
- WAF: ~$5-20/month (Web ACL + rules)
- Route 53: ~$1-2/month (health checks, hosted zone)
- **Total Infrastructure: ~$27-43/month**

---

## Monitoring & Observability

### CloudWatch Metrics (Automatic)

**Standard Metrics:**
- Requests (count)
- BytesDownloaded (sum)
- BytesUploaded (sum)
- TotalErrorRate (%)
- 4xxErrorRate (%)
- 5xxErrorRate (%)

**Additional Metrics:**
- CacheHitRate (%)
- OriginLatency (ms)

### CloudWatch Alarms (Recommended - Not Configured)

```yaml
# Example alarms to add
HighErrorRateAlarm:
  Threshold: 4xxErrorRate > 5%
  Period: 5 minutes

Origin5xxErrorAlarm:
  Threshold: 5xxErrorRate > 1%
  Period: 5 minutes

LowCacheHitRateAlarm:
  Threshold: CacheHitRate < 80%
  Period: 1 hour
```

### Access Logs
- **Location:** S3 bucket (maximus-cloudfront-logs-...)
- **Delivery:** Within 1-2 hours of request
- **Retention:** Per bucket lifecycle policy
- **Analysis:** Athena, EMR, or log analysis tools

---

## Operational Considerations

### Content Updates
```bash
# Upload new content to S3
aws s3 cp index.html s3://macp-prod-us-east-1-cloudfront-content/admin/

# Invalidate CloudFront cache (if needed for immediate update)
aws cloudfront create-invalidation \
  --distribution-id E1A2B3C4D5E6F7 \
  --paths "/admin/index.html"
```

**Invalidation Costs:**
- First 1,000 paths/month: Free
- Additional paths: $0.005 per path
- Use versioned filenames to avoid invalidations (e.g., `app.v2.js`)

### Monitoring Distribution Status
```bash
# Check deployment status
aws cloudfront get-distribution --id E1A2B3C4D5E6F7 \
  --query 'Distribution.Status'

# Possible statuses:
# - InProgress (deploying)
# - Deployed (ready)
```

### Disaster Recovery
- **Failover Time:** 60 seconds (Route 53 health check + DNS TTL)
- **Method:** Route 53 updates DNS to point to DR CloudFront distribution
- **Manual Trigger:** Disable primary region health check
- **Automatic:** Health check detects Connect instance failure

---

## Comparison to Existing Distribution

| Property | Existing (E28175TFKZ0RM5) | New Template | Match? |
|----------|---------------------------|--------------|--------|
| Aliases | 6 domains | 6 domains | ✅ |
| Origins | 1 S3 origin (/admin path) | 3 S3 origins (/admin, /agent, /chat) | ⚠️ More origins |
| OAC | Yes (E17BX9L2HH0V15) | Yes (new OAC per region) | ✅ |
| TLS Version | TLSv1.3_2025 | TLSv1.3_2025 | ✅ |
| HTTP Version | http2 | http2 | ✅ |
| IPv6 | Enabled | Enabled | ✅ |
| Compression | Enabled | Enabled | ✅ |
| Cache Policy | 658327ea-f89d-4fab-a63d-7e88639e58f6 | Same | ✅ |
| Geo-Restriction | US whitelist | US whitelist | ✅ |
| WAF | Same Web ACL | Same Web ACL | ✅ |
| Logging | Enabled (prefix: [dist-id]) | Enabled (prefix: prod-[region]-admin-portal/) | ⚠️ Different prefix |
| Price Class | PriceClass_All | PriceClass_All | ✅ |
| ViewerProtocol | redirect-to-https | redirect-to-https | ✅ |
| AllowedMethods | GET, HEAD | GET, HEAD | ✅ |

**Key Differences:**
1. **More Origins:** Template has 3 S3 origins (admin, agent, chat) vs 1 in existing
2. **Log Prefix:** More descriptive prefix for easier log organization
3. **New OAC:** Each region gets its own OAC (best practice)

**Architecture Improvement:**
- Existing: All traffic to /admin path
- New: Separate origins for each service (future-ready for API Gateway)

---

## Future Enhancements

### Short-term (Next Phase)
1. **Add Cache Behaviors** for path-based routing when APIs deployed
2. **Custom Error Pages** for better user experience (404, 403, 500)
3. **CloudWatch Alarms** for error rates and cache performance
4. **DefaultRootObject** if needed for cleaner URLs

### Medium-term
5. **Lambda@Edge** for authentication headers or URL rewriting
6. **Origin Shield** if origin load becomes concern
7. **Real-time Logs** for instant monitoring (if needed)
8. **Origin Groups** for automatic origin failover (vs DNS failover)

### Long-term
9. **Field-Level Encryption** if handling sensitive form data
10. **Signed URLs/Cookies** if content needs access control beyond geo
11. **Continuous Deployment** for safer production updates

---

## Questions to Confirm

1. ✅ **3 S3 origins acceptable?** (admin, agent, chat) vs 1 origin?
2. ✅ **Log prefix format acceptable?** `prod-[region]-admin-portal/`
3. ✅ **No cache behaviors initially?** Will add when API Gateway deployed
4. ✅ **TLS 1.3 minimum acceptable?** May exclude very old clients
5. ✅ **All edge locations (PriceClass_All)?** Highest cost but best performance
6. ✅ **No custom error pages initially?** Can add later
7. ✅ **No default root object?** Users must request `/index.html` explicitly

---

## Deployment Readiness Checklist

**Prerequisites:**
- ✅ ACM certificate validated (e925eda1-90b5-4607-8ad2-c2e924a3bb71)
- ✅ S3 bucket exists (will be created by stack)
- ✅ CloudFront logging bucket exists (maximus-cloudfront-logs-417886991978-us-east-1)
- ✅ WAF Web ACL exists (FMManagedWebACLV2-...)
- ✅ Content ready to upload (/admin/index.html)

**Post-Deployment:**
- [ ] Upload content to S3
- [ ] Test CloudFront access (*.cloudfront.net domain)
- [ ] Configure Route 53 DNS records (separate stack)
- [ ] Test custom domains
- [ ] Verify geo-restriction (test from non-US IP)
- [ ] Monitor CloudWatch metrics
- [ ] Review access logs after 2 hours

---

## Ready to Deploy?

Template creates CloudFront distributions that:
- ✅ Match existing distribution security and performance settings
- ✅ Support all 6 custom domains
- ✅ Use modern OAC (not legacy OAI)
- ✅ Enforce TLS 1.3 and HTTPS
- ✅ Include WAF protection
- ✅ Geo-restrict to US only
- ✅ Ready for future API Gateway integration (3 additional origins)
- ✅ Optimized for static content caching
